problem_statement: Long-term users and those with extensive libraries are frustrated that Spotify's algorithms (shuffle, recommendations, autoplay) repeatedly play the same known songs, hindering new music discovery and making the listening experience feel stale and less exploratory.
root_cause_hypothesis: The current recommendation and shuffle algorithms may be over-optimizing for engagement with familiar content, creating a feedback loop that prioritizes known songs and artists over true novelty and diverse exploration, leading to a perceived lack of randomness and discovery.
affected_users: Primarily long-term and power users with large music libraries (thousands of liked songs/playlist entries) who value music discovery, but also impacts general users experiencing repetition in autoplay and curated playlists.
evidence: My discover weekly is full of songs I have already in my playlists, and even my playlists or liked songs with over thousands of songs play the same songs over and over in shuffle mode...
Spotify basically pushes the same songs to everyone. When you go out of your playlists, or use their new smart shuffle feature, you end up with the same songs...
I want the recommendations to be new!!! Instead it's just all songs i already know and are in other playlists. I want to discover new music!!!
I keep having the same damn songs played over and over again when I let Spotify 'autoplay', even the ones I'm hiding from the list, they keep coming back!
The discover weekly playlists are so irrelevant to what I listen to & artist radio doesn’t suggest enough new music I haven’t heard by the artists.
product_opportunities: Re-evaluate and enhance core recommendation algorithms to prioritize novelty and diversity for engaged users.
Improve shuffle functionality to ensure genuine randomness across large playlists.
Optimize autoplay and radio features to consistently introduce new, relevant music.
Provide users with more granular control over recommendation freshness vs. familiarity.
recommended_features: "True Random Shuffle" mode for playlists, ensuring all songs have an equal chance of playing.
"Discovery Mode" toggle for recommendation surfaces (e.g., Discover Weekly, Daily Mixes) to explicitly prioritize new-to-user tracks.
Algorithm update to reduce repetition of recently played or already-liked songs in discovery contexts.
Enhanced "Smart Shuffle" or "Enhance" feature that consistently introduces truly new, relevant tracks.
User setting to define a "novelty threshold" for recommendations.
success_metrics: Increase in "new-to-user" song saves/likes from algorithmic playlists (e.g., Discover Weekly, Daily Mixes).
Decrease in user complaints related to song repetition in shuffle, autoplay, and recommendations.
Increase in the average number of unique tracks played per user session for power users.
Improvement in user sentiment scores related to music discovery and personalization.

problem_statement: Users are actively comparing Spotify to competitors like Apple Music, often needing to justify their choice. While Spotify's discovery, social features, and seamless experience are highly valued, competitive offerings (e.g., sound quality, ecosystem integration) create decision friction and require Spotify to continuously reinforce its unique value proposition.
root_cause_hypothesis: The competitive landscape, particularly with Apple Music's ecosystem integration and lossless audio offerings, prompts users to critically evaluate their streaming service choice. Spotify's core strengths are recognized, but the perception of competitive advantages in other areas necessitates continuous reinforcement and enhancement of Spotify's unique value.
affected_users: Users actively evaluating streaming services, including existing Spotify Premium users defending their choice and potential new users weighing options. This segment is highly engaged with music discovery and social features.
evidence: Spotify, on the other hand, has an unmatched recommendation engine. It always seems to know exactly what I want to hear, even when I don’t. The Discover Weekly and Release Radar playlists have introduced me to so many new artists I would’ve never found otherwise.

Social features? I share songs, playlists, and stories with friends constantly. Apple’s sharing is straight-up prehistoric. ... Spotify Wrapped alone mogs Apple’s sad little year-end recap into oblivion. Every year. No contest.
Spotify Connect is god-tier. Phone → laptop → car → speakers without skipping a beat. Seamless. Apple still feels clunky in comparison.
Apple Musics algorithm is miles below Spotify, I discover double the amount of new songs on Spotify in 3 months than I have in a year of Apple Music.
I really want to use Apple Music since its interface is really nice, but I just can't seem to find any workarounds to make the Apple Music discovery system work well.
product_opportunities: Reinforce and enhance Spotify's core differentiators in music discovery and personalization.
Further develop and promote social and community features that foster user connection and sharing.
Improve the seamless multi-device experience (Spotify Connect) to maintain its 'god-tier' status.
Address perceived gaps in audio quality (e.g., HiFi) while educating users on its practical impact.
Leverage and amplify user-generated content and advocacy through features like Spotify Wrapped and listening stats.
recommended_features: Enhanced AI-driven discovery features beyond existing playlists (e.g., more dynamic Daily Mixes, AI DJ improvements).
Expanded social sharing capabilities, including richer media sharing and collaborative listening experiences.
Continued investment in Spotify Connect reliability and integration with new devices/platforms.
Clear communication and potential rollout of a HiFi/Lossless audio tier with practical benefits highlighted.
More interactive and personalized year-end/seasonal listening statistics and shareable content.
success_metrics: Increase in engagement with discovery playlists (e.g., Discover Weekly, Daily Mixes) by 10%.
Increase in social sharing actions (e.g., sharing songs/playlists to social media, Blend usage) by 15%.
Reduction in Premium churn rate by 0.5%.
Improvement in Net Promoter Score (NPS) by 3 points.
Increase in Spotify Connect usage frequency by 10%.

problem_statement: Independent artists lack effective, integrated channels within Spotify to gain exposure and receive feedback, leading listeners and artists to create manual, external community-driven solutions for discovering and promoting emerging music.
root_cause_hypothesis: Spotify's existing discovery algorithms and editorial processes may not adequately serve the long tail of independent artists or the specific user desire for human-curated, community-vetted music discovery, prompting users to build their own systems outside the platform.
affected_users: Independent artists seeking exposure and feedback, listeners actively looking for new and emerging music, and community organizers who curate and manage shared playlists for discovery.
evidence: Every week, I update the Reddit Artist Playlist with new music from independent artists across Reddit. If you’ve got a track on Spotify that deserves some ears, drop the link in the comments below — I listen to every submission.
The playlist is capped at 50 tracks and rotates weekly to keep things dynamic and discovery-friendly. If you're into supporting emerging music, this is your kind of thread.
🎧 Stream + Save the Playlist: [link] (Saving the playlist helps boost visibility for every artist featured.)
I was thinking it might be fun to compile the recommendations in one Spotify playlist that anyone can listen to and extract songs they like into their own playlists.
🚀 Want more in-depth feedback? Try ReviewQueue.app — a new platform for music discovery and honest reviews. Submit your track, get feedback, earn XP, and discover new artists before they blow up.
product_opportunities: Develop official tools for community-driven music curation and discovery within Spotify.
Create dedicated pathways for independent artists to submit music for community review and playlist consideration.
Enhance the visibility and discoverability of emerging artists within the Spotify ecosystem.
Facilitate direct feedback and interaction between artists and listeners around new releases.
recommended_features: A 'Community Submissions' feature where artists can submit tracks to public, user-curated playlists, with built-in tools for curators to review and select.
An 'Emerging Artists Hub' or dedicated section within Spotify, showcasing community-vetted independent music and active community curators.
In-app tools for playlist curators to manage submissions, provide feedback, and promote their community playlists more effectively.
A 'Listener Feedback' mechanism for artists to receive direct, constructive reviews on submitted tracks, potentially with gamification elements (e.g., XP, badges).
success_metrics: Increase in the number of independent artists submitting music through official Spotify channels.
Increase in engagement (streams, saves, shares) on community-curated playlists.
Growth in the number of active community curators creating and managing playlists.
Reduction in external community-driven playlist initiatives (e.g., on Reddit) as users migrate to in-app solutions.
Increase in the discoverability metric for independent artists (e.g., average new listener reach).

problem_statement: Users, particularly on the free tier, are experiencing a significantly degraded product experience due to excessive and intrusive ads, severe playback restrictions, and aggressive premium upsells, leading to frustration and uninstalls. Some premium users also feel the value proposition is diminishing, especially regarding new features like audiobooks.
root_cause_hypothesis: An aggressive monetization strategy for the free tier, aimed at driving premium conversions, is inadvertently making the free experience unusable and alienating users, while also failing to clearly communicate the value of premium features like audiobooks.
affected_users: Primarily free users who are experiencing high ad loads and restrictive playback. Also, some premium users who feel the value of their subscription (e.g., audiobooks) is not as advertised or expected.
evidence: Now I get 1.5 mins of ads every 10 minutes. That is way too much. I feel like, instead of making it more attractive to buy the premium version, spotify is just trying to make the free version completely unusable.

without premium you can't even skip or pick any of the music, you can only use shuffle or play it the playlist regularly but with my playlist I have a bunch of the same people over and over and that gets annoying...

As a free user, I find this app frustrating. ...the constant ads and repeated premium pop-ups make the experience annoying. What bothers me most is that I cannot always listen to my favorite songs the way I want because of the restrictions.
I had premium and it advertises audiobooks with your subscription, but only allows so many hours before it wants more money. cancelled premium bc I only listen to podcasts that have ads built in...

The biggest problem is that I can only choose a few songs, and then Spotify starts playing random songs that I didn't even select. It becomes frustrating when I just want to listen to my own playlist.
product_opportunities: Re-evaluate and optimize the free tier ad experience (frequency, duration, relevance) to be less intrusive.
Improve the perceived utility and flexibility of the free tier's music playback features to reduce frustration.
Clearly define and communicate the value proposition of Spotify Premium, especially for new features like audiobooks.
Explore alternative monetization models or a 'freemium light' tier that offers a better balance.
recommended_features: Dynamic ad frequency/duration adjustments based on user engagement and session length to prevent ad fatigue.
Limited 'on-demand' plays or a certain number of skips per hour/day for free users on specific playlists.
Enhanced in-app messaging and transparent terms for Premium benefits, especially for audiobooks entitlements.
A 'podcast-only' ad-free mini-subscription or a more generous, time-limited free trial for premium features.
success_metrics: Increase in free user retention rate by X%.
Increase in free-to-premium conversion rate by Y%.
Decrease in negative sentiment related to 'ads' and 'restrictions' in app store reviews and community forums.
Reduction in uninstalls among free users by Z%.

theme: AI DJ Recommendation Quality & AI Music Concerns
problem_statement: Spotify Premium users are dissatisfied with the AI DJ's music recommendations, finding them irrelevant to their taste, repetitive, or containing undesirable AI-generated music, which diminishes the value of the feature and their overall discovery experience.
root_cause_hypothesis: The AI DJ's personalization algorithm may not be effectively interpreting user taste profiles or balancing familiarity with novelty, leading to off-target recommendations. Additionally, the presence of AI-generated music, perceived as low quality or forced, is causing user frustration and distrust.
affected_users: Primarily Spotify Premium subscribers who have access to and are attempting to use the AI DJ feature, especially those who rely on Spotify for personalized music discovery and value human-created content.
evidence: I just saw on my laptop today that Spotify has an "AI" DJ now. I tried it out and the new music recommendations were absolutely terrible.

Lately I’ve felt like Spotify’s AI DJ is completely ignoring my actual music taste. It used to play tracks that matched my vibe perfectly, but now it throws in random songs and genres I’ve never listened to (and clearly don’t like).

Absolute GARBAGE AI music appeared in my Discover Weekly today for the first time. I've read posts regarding people coming across AI music on Spotify, but for it to now be forced upon me is insane when I'm already paying a premium.

After being greeted with an uncanny voice in my language, it started playing a selection of horrible music well outside my taste, and the voice of the singers felt pretty much AI generated too. It really feels like they wanna drive real artists out of the streaming platforms and turn everything into AI music, where Spotify don't have to pay royalties in the end.
product_opportunities: Enhance the personalization and recommendation accuracy of the AI DJ.
Provide users with greater control and feedback mechanisms for the AI DJ's selections.
Address user concerns and provide transparency regarding the presence and identification of AI-generated music.
Re-evaluate the balance between discovery and familiarity within the AI DJ's algorithm.
recommended_features: Implement more granular feedback options within the AI DJ interface (e.g., 'dislike this song/artist/genre', 'play more like this', 'skip and explain why').
Introduce AI DJ 'tuning' settings (e.g., a slider for 'familiarity vs. discovery', genre/mood filters, or 'exclude AI-generated music').
Develop a clear policy and user-facing indicators for AI-generated music, potentially allowing users to filter it out from all discovery features.
Conduct an audit and refinement of the AI DJ's underlying recommendation algorithm to improve relevance and reduce 'random' selections.
success_metrics: Increase in average AI DJ session duration by X%.
Increase in 'thumbs up' or 'save' actions within AI DJ by Y%.
Decrease in negative sentiment mentions related to 'AI DJ' and 'recommendations' in user feedback channels by Z%.
Improvement in user satisfaction scores for music discovery features.

problem_statement: Users are experiencing frequent and disruptive app freezes, unexpected pauses, and crashes, making Spotify unreliable for continuous music playback and causing significant frustration, especially for Premium subscribers.
root_cause_hypothesis: The issues likely stem from client-side bugs related to memory management, background process handling, or specific device/OS interactions, leading to instability and interruptions in core playback functionality.
affected_users: All users, but particularly Premium subscribers who expect a seamless, ad-free, and uninterrupted listening experience across various usage scenarios (e.g., offline, multitasking).
evidence: It plays a song or two, then pauses altogether rather than moving on to the next song in the queue.
It randomly freezes my music and I usually have to unpause it a few times to get it playing again.
every time I want to play a game, my music stops and Spotify decides to restart my entire song
I have been trying to open the app for the past three days and it keeps reading something went wrong, try again.
Whenever I open Spotify, it gets stuck on the loading screen and eventually tells me to check my internet connection.
product_opportunities: Improve core app stability and reliability for uninterrupted playback.
Enhance background process management and multitasking robustness.
Provide clearer diagnostic information and error handling for users.
recommended_features: Dedicated 'stability sprint' to address top crash and ANR (Application Not Responding) issues.
Implement more robust background playback services to prevent OS termination or conflicts.
Improve in-app error messaging to provide specific guidance instead of generic 'something went wrong.'
Enhance client-side logging and crash reporting for better debugging.
success_metrics: Decrease in app crash rate by X%.
Decrease in reported playback interruption incidents by Y%.
Increase in average session duration by Z%.
Improvement in App Store/Google Play ratings related to 'stability' or 'reliability.'

problem_statement: Independent artists struggle to understand and effectively leverage Spotify's algorithmic recommendation system to gain visibility and grow their audience, leading them to seek external, often paid, strategies to 'crack' the algorithm.
root_cause_hypothesis: Spotify's existing tools and documentation within Spotify for Artists may lack sufficient transparency, actionable guidance, and integrated promotional capabilities for independent artists to organically influence algorithmic discovery without resorting to complex or costly third-party methods.
affected_users: Independent artists, small to medium-sized music creators, and their teams who are actively trying to promote their music and grow their audience on Spotify.
evidence: We pulled together a no-fluff guide for artists on how Spotify actually recommends music and what you can *control* as an indie. It’s vendor-agnostic and links to official sources.

John, an independent artist... has revealed his step-by-step strategy for cracking Spotify’s algorithm. Using targeted ads and strategic play-listing, John successfully got his latest track featured on Discover Weekly...
Discovery Mode (pros/cons): Can boost exposure in personalized contexts, but it comes with a royalty trade-off widely reported as \~30% reduction...
Spotify has just recently added one of our songs to Discover Weekly and it resulted in our most streams in a single day... We've been pushing our different marketing tactics hard... to get here.
Signals that seem to matter: saves, skips/finishes, session context, and listening similarity—i.e., real listener satisfaction over time, not just one-off spikes.
product_opportunities: Enhance transparency and provide clearer, official guidance on algorithmic mechanics for artists.
Develop integrated tools within Spotify for Artists that help artists generate the 'signals' crucial for algorithmic discovery.
Streamline and optimize existing paid promotional tools (Discovery Mode, Showcase) for better artist understanding and ROI.
Facilitate official knowledge sharing and community support for artists seeking algorithmic growth strategies.
recommended_features: **Algorithmic Insights Dashboard in S4A**: A dedicated section providing a 'health score' for tracks, explaining their algorithmic performance based on key signals (saves, skips, completion rate, listener similarity) and offering actionable tips.
**Algorithmic Readiness Score/Checklist**: A tool for artists to assess an unreleased or new track's potential for algorithmic pickup, with suggestions for optimizing its chances.

**Integrated 'Engagement Boost' Campaigns**: A simplified, in-app tool within S4A allowing artists to run small, targeted campaigns to generate initial, high-quality engagement signals from relevant listeners.

**Official Best Practices & Case Studies Library**: A regularly updated resource within S4A featuring successful artist strategies for algorithmic growth, including effective use of Spotify's own tools.
**Discovery Mode/Showcase ROI Calculator & A/B Testing**: Tools to help artists understand the potential impact, royalty trade-offs, and optimize their paid promotional campaigns.
success_metrics: Increase in the number of independent artists achieving algorithmic playlist placement (e.g., Discover Weekly, Radio).
Reduction in artists seeking external, paid services for algorithmic promotion (e.g., tracking mentions of third-party tools in artist forums).
Increase in artist satisfaction scores related to 'growth' and 'discovery tools' in S4A surveys.
Higher adoption and positive feedback on new S4A features related to algorithmic guidance.

problem_statement: Users are actively creating and sharing multi-volume series of curated mixes or playlists, often linking them manually outside of Spotify, indicating a desire for better native tools to organize, present, and discover such sequential content.
root_cause_hypothesis: Spotify's current playlist and mix features are primarily designed for single, standalone collections of music, lacking native functionality to group, sequence, or present multiple related playlists as a cohesive series. This forces users to use external platforms and manual linking to manage and share their multi-volume creations.
affected_users: Highly engaged users and community curators who create and share extensive, multi-part music collections, often acting as informal tastemakers.
evidence: **Daily Listening:** [Sample Sessions Vol II]( | *Curated by* u/j_like [Spotify Playlist of Vol. 5 Mixes]( | [Spotify Playlist of Vol. 4 Mixes]( | [Spotify Playlist of Vol. 3 Mixes]( | [Spotify Playlist of Vol. 2 Mixes]( | [Spotify Playlist of Vol. 1 Mixes](
Users are sharing 'Daily Listening' mixes curated by themselves or other users.
The consistent linking of 'Vol. 1 Mixes' through 'Vol. 5 Mixes' demonstrates a desire to present playlists as a sequential series.
The 'Curated by u/...' tag highlights user-driven curation and community sharing of these mixes.
product_opportunities: Enhance community curation and content creation tools within Spotify.
Improve discoverability and organization of user-generated content series.
Foster deeper engagement among power users and curators by providing better native support for their activities.
Leverage user-generated series to create new content discovery pathways.
recommended_features: **Playlist Series Feature**: Allow users to group multiple playlists into a named series, with clear navigation between volumes (e.g., 'Vol. 1 of 5').
**Curator Profiles/Pages**: Enhance user profiles to better showcase their curated series, making it easier for others to follow their work.
**In-App Sharing for Series**: Provide a dedicated sharing mechanism for an entire playlist series, rather than just individual playlists.
**'Community Curated' Hub**: Create a dedicated section within Spotify to highlight popular or trending user-curated series.
success_metrics: Increase in the number of playlist series created by users.
Increase in engagement with playlist series (e.g., streams per series, unique listeners per series).
Increase in followers for curators who create series.
Reduction in external links to Spotify playlists on platforms like Reddit (indicating adoption of native sharing).

problem_statement: Users are experiencing significant degradation in the functionality, reliability, and aesthetic appeal of the new Spotify music widget, leading to frustration, decreased usage, and a perception of a worse overall Spotify experience.
root_cause_hypothesis: The recent widget update introduced technical regressions (e.g., not opening, unresponsiveness, incorrect content display) and removed preferred size/layout options, forcing users into a less functional and aesthetically displeasing experience without providing alternatives.
affected_users: Highly active users who rely on home screen widgets for quick control and access to their music and podcasts, particularly those who valued smaller, more compact widget sizes and consistent performance.
evidence: the new music widget absolutely sucks. the old one worked great. but this "new and improved" one... won't even open 80% of the time and when it does it opens to the complete wrong song or playlist that you were playing.
The new spotify widget is trash, it's not as responsive, and if you haven't played music in a while it'll go "offline" and you'll have to open the app to start any music.

this recent update where it's informing us that the 4x1 widget is no longer going to be available and we have to switch to the bigger 4x2 widget... I prefer having my home screens locked with similar applications stacked for a more organised look. This new widget is not even aesthetically pleasing.
got rid of the 1x3 widget for no reason. I don't want to use half my screen for the 2x high widget. Honestly makes the whole experience so much worse.
Zero functionality. Most of the time it doesn't even load or else it continuously directs me straight to some random podcast episode even though I'm actively listening to music.
product_opportunities: Restore core widget functionality and reliability to meet or exceed previous performance standards.
Offer greater customization and choice for widget sizes and layouts to accommodate diverse user preferences.
Improve widget responsiveness and ensure it accurately reflects current playback status.
Address aesthetic concerns by providing more visually appealing and space-efficient widget options.
recommended_features: Reintroduce smaller, more compact widget sizes (e.g., 1x3, 4x1) as an option.
Fix critical bugs preventing the widget from opening, responding, or displaying the correct content.
Ensure the widget remains 'online' and responsive without requiring the main app to be opened first.
Implement a user setting to choose between different widget designs or layouts (e.g., classic vs. new).
success_metrics: Increase in daily active widget users by 20%
Reduction in negative App Store/Google Play reviews mentioning 'widget' by 50%
Improvement in widget interaction rate (taps/skips) by 15%
Increase in overall app satisfaction scores for users who previously used widgets

problem_statement: Users highly value Spotify's lean-back, continuously evolving personalized music mixes (like Daily Mixes and AI DJ) that balance familiar songs with new discoveries. They struggle to find an equivalent experience on other platforms or feel Spotify's current offerings (e.g., Smart Shuffle, geo-restricted AI DJ) are insufficient, leading to frustration and potential churn.
root_cause_hypothesis: Spotify's advanced personalization algorithms, particularly those powering Daily Mixes and AI DJ, create a unique and highly sticky lean-back listening experience. Inconsistencies in feature availability (e.g., AI DJ geo-restrictions) or perceived repetitiveness in other personalized features (e.g., Smart Shuffle) create gaps that lead users to seek alternatives or express dissatisfaction.
affected_users: Highly engaged Spotify users who prefer a lean-back, continuous listening experience, value a blend of known and new music, and may be considering or have already switched to competitor platforms. This includes users who listen in specific contexts like driving.
evidence: I let Spotify make and customize my daily mix every day and I was content with it. Usually it was like 70% music I listened to before, 20% songs I listened to a few times but not regularly, and like 10% new songs.

I’m addicted to the **Spotifys AI DJ**. I actually love the dumbness of that one spotify feature. I’m looking for the Apple Music equivalent of an **ever-changing mix that weaves my liked songs with new recommendations.**
It perfectly balances songs I already love with 'discovery' tracks. I don't have to pick a playlist; I just hit play and it goes forever.

I’ve been a long-time Spotify user, but there’s one thing I feel is really missing: a feature like Deezer’s Flow or Apple Music’s Radio. These features create an endless stream of music based on your taste, blending your favorites with new recommendations effortlessly.
When I start it over, I often notice songs repeating instead of giving me a fresh experience. I’ve heard a lot about the AI DJ, but unfortunately, it’s not available in my country.
product_opportunities: Enhance and standardize the 'lean-back' discovery experience across all Spotify users and regions.
Leverage Spotify's core strength in personalization to further differentiate from competitors' 'radio' or 'flow' features.
Address feature parity and availability issues for highly valued personalized experiences like AI DJ.
Re-evaluate and potentially evolve past successful discovery features like Time Capsule.
recommended_features: Global rollout of the AI DJ feature to all countries where Spotify operates.

Develop an 'Infinite Mix' or 'Personalized Radio 2.0' feature that offers a continuously evolving, highly varied stream of music blending user favorites with new recommendations, with reduced repetition.
Introduce more contextual or mood-based dynamic mixes that adapt to user activity or time of day.
Reintroduce and modernize the 'Time Capsule' feature to help users rediscover forgotten favorites in a dynamic playlist format.
success_metrics: Increase in daily active users (DAU) engaging with personalized mixes/stations by 10%.
Increase in average listening time per session for personalized mixes by 15%.
Reduction in churn rate for users who frequently use personalized mixes by 5%.
Increase in 'save to library' or 'like' actions originating from personalized mix recommendations by 20%.
Improvement in user sentiment scores related to 'discovery' and 'personalization' by 0.5 points on a 5-point scale.

problem_statement: Users feel Spotify's current discovery mechanisms promote superficial, song-based listening, preventing them from deeply engaging with artists and albums, and hindering their ability to break out of established taste bubbles for meaningful new music discovery.
root_cause_hypothesis: Spotify's algorithms and UI are primarily optimized for individual track consumption and short-term engagement, leading to repetitive recommendations and a lack of features that encourage intentional, album-centric exploration or provide rich context for deeper understanding.
affected_users: Long-term Premium subscribers, music enthusiasts, and creative professionals who desire a more profound and intentional music discovery experience beyond algorithmic playlists and individual tracks.
evidence: when it came to discovering new stuff? It didn’t work for me. Not properly. I’d stumble on a new song here and there, sure... but it was always just that, one song. And one song doesn’t always pull you into an artist.
I ended up stuck in this loop, going back to the same artists I’ve liked for years. Artists I already know inside-out. It was frustrating. I couldn’t break out of that bubble.

I’ve found myself listening to entire albums all the way through, actively researching and looking into new and old artists, reading and watching interviews and generally collecting music in a more intentional and meaningful way.

streaming was great at first. But something about it now feels... hollow? Like a fast food version of music. No liner notes. No sense of discovery. Just algorithmic playlists and the same old tracks getting pushed.

I’ve developed the habit of listening to individual songs rather than exploring entire discographies... instead of staying with that band and exploring their albums or deeper cuts, I move on to the next artist.
product_opportunities: Re-introduce intentional, curated discovery experiences that prioritize full album listening.
Enhance artist and album pages with rich contextual information and deep-dive tools.
Develop features that challenge users' existing taste and encourage exploration outside their comfort zone.
Foster a sense of 'collecting' or 'curating' music within the platform.
recommended_features: "Album of the Week Club" or "Discovery Journey" feature, presenting a curated album for focused listening.
Expanded artist profiles with discography deep-dives, influences, related artists, and editorial content (reviews, interviews, liner notes).
"Challenge Mode" for discovery, offering albums or artists intentionally outside the user's typical listening habits.
Interactive "Music History Timelines" or "Genre Exploration Paths" that guide users through foundational works.
success_metrics: Increase in average full album plays per user per month.
Increase in time spent on artist and album detail pages.
Higher engagement rates with new discovery features (e.g., completion rate of 'Album of the Week').
Increase in the number of unique artists/albums saved or followed after engaging with discovery features.
Improved user sentiment scores related to 'music discovery' and 'depth of engagement'.

theme: Algorithmic Discovery Gap in Hi-Res Audio
problem_statement: Audiophile users are migrating to or supplementing Spotify with high-resolution audio platforms like Qobuz, but they deeply miss Spotify's superior algorithmic music discovery, particularly artist-based radio and personalized mixes, which Qobuz's current offerings fail to provide adequately.
root_cause_hypothesis: Spotify's lack of a high-resolution audio offering drives audiophiles to competitors, where they then experience a significant degradation in algorithmic discovery quality compared to Spotify, leading them to seek or build third-party solutions to bridge this gap.
affected_users: Audiophiles and music enthusiasts who prioritize high-resolution audio quality, are willing to pay for premium services (e.g., Qobuz subscribers), and are technically savvy enough to seek out or build third-party solutions for discovery.
evidence: Spotify lets you create a station from any artist and it just works — but Qobuz doesn't have anything close.
The problem: Qobuz has an incredible hi-res library but no way to create a station from an artist. Meanwhile, Spotify nails discovery and radio — but at the cost of audio quality.
So I built Sonic Oracle (sonicoracle.ai) — a music discovery engine that brings Spotify-level artist radio to the Qobuz hi-res library.
However, one feature I miss is their mixes and how I was able to find artists like artists that I like.
If you look up a small artist, go to song radio, it replies with 'radio silence'. Spotify was, for all its failings, much better at recommending small artists.
product_opportunities: Introduce a competitive high-resolution audio offering to retain audiophile users.
Reinforce and enhance Spotify's core algorithmic discovery features, especially artist radio, to maintain a clear competitive advantage.
Improve the accuracy and inclusivity of the AI DJ and other discovery algorithms, particularly for niche artists and diverse genres.
Explore integrations or partnerships that could bring Spotify's discovery prowess to high-res ecosystems.
recommended_features: Launch a 'Spotify Hi-Fi' tier with lossless and high-resolution audio options.
Enhance 'Artist Radio' with advanced filtering (e.g., genre-aware, vocal gender matching) and multi-source recommendation algorithms.
Conduct a thorough audit and improvement cycle for the AI DJ's genre classification and recommendation logic, especially for niche and diverse artists.
Develop more robust discovery tools and algorithmic weighting for emerging and small artists across all discovery surfaces.
success_metrics: Reduction in churn rate among users identified as audiophiles or high-res audio listeners.
Increase in 'Artist Radio' session duration and 'add to library/playlist' rates.
Improvement in AI DJ satisfaction scores and reduction in negative feedback related to genre descriptions.
Growth in the number of unique small/niche artists discovered and saved by users.
Increase in overall engagement metrics (e.g., daily active users, time spent listening) for users who previously engaged with high-res audio content.
